---
title: Claim Coverage Revision Baseline
---

## Abstract

Our model achieves 92% accuracy on standard benchmarks, consistent with prior
results reported by Smith et al. [@smith2023].

## Introduction

Deep neural networks achieve 95% accuracy on image classification tasks [@lecun2015].

Our method is 2x faster than baseline approaches, as measured in controlled
experiments [@jones2022].

## Methods

We use standard cross-entropy loss with Adam optimizer.

## Results

Figure 1 shows the pipeline architecture.

The method outperforms all prior approaches on three benchmark datasets [@wang2021].

Our model achieves 92% accuracy on the CIFAR benchmark.

## Discussion

These results confirm the advantages of the proposed architecture.

## References

- LeCun, Y. et al. (2015). Deep learning. Nature.
- Smith, A. et al. (2023). Accuracy benchmarks. ICML.
- Jones, B. et al. (2022). Speed comparison. NeurIPS.
- Wang, C. et al. (2021). Benchmark evaluation. CVPR.
